title: Standard 6-stream M&A DD checklist
text: Financial: 3 years audited financials + interim + projections, working capital target, EBITDA adjustments / quality-of-earnings, debt + cash + working-capital, off-balance-sheet items. Legal: corporate organization, material contracts, litigation, regulatory, IP assignments. IP: patent + trademark + copyright register, OSS compliance, trade-secret protection, IP assignment from employees + contractors. Customer: concentration analysis, contract change-of-control, satisfaction signals. HR: employment agreements, equity / option pool, accrued PTO, classification (1099 vs W2), WARN exposure, key-person retention. Tax: federal/state/local returns, NOL carry-forward + §382 study, sales / use tax exposure, payroll tax, R&D credit, transfer pricing.

title: R&W insurance scope
text: Representations & Warranties insurance covers undisclosed breaches of seller reps in the purchase agreement. Standard scope: 10% of deal value (subject to deal size), 1-3% premium, 3-6 year tail. Exclusions: known issues (disclosed in disclosure schedules), specific carve-outs (e.g. PFAS environmental, cyber, transfer-pricing), forward-looking statements. Buyer must conduct adequate DD to satisfy underwriter.

title: NOL §382 limitation
text: After an 'ownership change' (>50% change in 5% shareholders over 3-yr testing period), the corporation's pre-change NOLs are limited annually to: long-term tax-exempt rate × value of corporation immediately before change. Built-in gains / losses adjustments apply. §382 study required to confirm utilization plan.

title: Federal WARN Act
text: Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act. Employers with 100+ employees must provide 60 days advance notice of: plant closing (>= 50 employees) OR mass layoff (>= 50 employees + 33% of workforce, or >= 500 regardless). Exceptions: faltering company, unforeseeable circumstance, natural disaster. State 'mini-WARN' laws (CA, NY, IL) have lower thresholds + longer notice. Damages: back pay + benefits for the violation period (up to 60 days).

title: Open-source license compatibility
text: Categories: permissive (MIT / BSD / Apache 2.0 / ISC) — generally compatible with proprietary distribution. Weak copyleft (LGPL / MPL) — must allow user to relink; modifications to library must be open-sourced. Strong copyleft (GPL v2 / GPL v3) — derivative works must be open-sourced (with exception for system libraries). Network copyleft (AGPL) — even SaaS distribution triggers copyleft. M&A red flag: GPL/AGPL code in proprietary product without proper isolation.

title: Customer concentration thresholds + treatment
text: Industry benchmarks: < 10% from single customer = healthy; 10-25% = monitor; > 25% from single OR > 50% from top-5 = material concentration. Treatment in M&A: re-trade price, escrow holdback, earnouts tied to retention, change-of-control consents + acceleration. Customer interviews / NPS / contract-renewal-rate diligence.

title: Change-of-control acceleration triggers
text: Common triggers: customer contracts (right to terminate / renegotiate), software licenses (especially Microsoft / Oracle / SAP — often require true-up audits on COC), leases (landlord consent), debt covenants (acceleration), executive change-in-control payments (golden parachutes — §280G analysis required if > 3× base compensation), option vesting acceleration (single-trigger vs double-trigger).

title: Key-person retention design
text: Standard retention packages: stay-bonus (cash payable at month X / Y / Z, e.g. 33/33/34% at 12/24/36 months), rollover-equity (founder rolls equity into NewCo + new vesting), earnout structures tied to performance + retention. Risk: founder leaving immediately post-close = enterprise-value impairment 10-30%+.

title: Golden parachute §280G
text: If executive receives payments contingent on a change-of-control > 3× base amount (5-year avg compensation), the excess is non-deductible to the corporation AND subject to 20% excise tax to the recipient. §280G analysis required pre-close; cleansing-vote shareholder approval may avoid the parachute treatment (private companies).

title: Quality-of-Earnings
text: Buyer-side or seller-side QoE report. Identifies: non-recurring items (one-time gains/losses, COVID impacts), aggressive accounting (revenue recognition cutoffs, capitalized expenses), management adjustments (owner perks, related-party), normalized EBITDA. Often becomes the basis for the purchase price + working capital adjustment.
